title: Mayor Walsh launches 'Sustainable Syracuse' to fight climate change and cut carbon emissions
summary: Mayor Ben Walsh launched the 'Sustainable Syracuse' initiative to reduce the city's carbon footprint and build climate resilience. The program received $100,000 from New York State Department of Environmental Conservation and will develop a greenhouse gas inventory and climate action plan, led by Syracuse's Division of Planning and Sustainability in partnership with C&S Companies.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation

political_signal.description: Mayor-led climate initiative with state funding support signals municipal commitment to sustainability and environmental resilience as a governance priority.

processing_notes: Department extracted as 'Division of Planning and Sustainability' (more specific than initial 'Department of Permits and Development'). Partnership with private consultant (C&S Companies) indicates hybrid delivery model. Earth Week announcement timing suggests strategic communications alignment.
